title: Docker cli compatability in format: can't evaluate field RunningFor in type shared.PsContainerOutput
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

`Podman` announced as a drop-in replacement for `docker` cli, but it fails to use `RunningFor` for format output of containers statistic.



**Steps to reproduce the issue:**
1. Run any container by `podman`: ```podman run -d nginx:alpine```
2. Request formatted list of containers with `.RunningFor` placeholder like:
```
podman ps --format "table {{.Names}}\t{{.Status}}\t{{.RunningFor}}\t{{.Image}}\t{{.Ports}}"
```

**Describe the results you received:**
Error:
```
$ podman ps --format "table {{.Names}}\t{{.Status}}\t{{.RunningFor}}\t{{.Image}}\t{{.Ports}}"
Names\tStatus\t\tImage\tPorts
Error: template: data:1:27: executing "data" at <.RunningFor>: can't evaluate field RunningFor in type shared.PsContainerOutput
```

**Describe the results you expected:**
Output same as got from `docker` cli:
```
$ sudo docker ps --format "table {{.Names}}\t{{.Status}}\t{{.RunningFor}}\t{{.Image}}\t{{.Ports}}"
NAMES                 STATUS                            CREATED             IMAGE               PORTS
condescending_gould   Up About a minute                 2 minutes ago       nginx:alpine        80/tcp
```

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**
Always reproducible

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Version:            1.8.1
RemoteAPI Version:  1
Go Version:         go1.14rc1
OS/Arch:            linux/amd64
```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**

```

  MemFree: 6739705856

      runc version 1.0.0-rc10
      commit: dc9208a3303feef5b3839f4323d9beb36df0a9dd

  SwapFree: 6264819712
```

```
$ rpm -q podman
```

comments.body: Looks like it's named `Created` in our structs. Should be easy enough to rename to `RunningFor`

It is not enough just rename field. Please look:
```
$ podman ps --format "table {{.Names}}\t{{.Status}}\t{{.Created}}\t{{.Image}}\t{{.Ports}}"
Names\tStatus\tCreated\tImage\tPorts
ghost\tUp 5 minutes ago\t5 minutes ago\tdocker.io/library/ghost:latest\t0.0.0.0:8080->80/tcp
nginx\tUp 7 minutes ago\t7 minutes ago\tdocker.io/library/nginx:latest\t0.0.0.0:8080->80/tcp
```
Formatting is completely broken.
If i remove `\t` it looks better:
```
$ podman ps --format "table {{.Names}} {{.Status}} {{.Created}} {{.Image}} {{.Ports}}"
Names   Status             Created         Image                            Ports
ghost   Up 6 minutes ago   6 minutes ago   docker.io/library/ghost:latest   0.0.0.0:8080->80/tcp
nginx   Up 8 minutes ago   8 minutes ago   docker.io/library/nginx:latest   0.0.0.0:8080->80/tcp
```
But still with some escaping artifacts like `->`
@Hubbitus Any chance you could try this with the podmanV2 work?
With podman-1.9.0 even `{{.NAMES}}` fails for me ;-(
Is this supposed to be case insensitive?
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
@juhp Have you tried with podman 2.0?  This is a major rewrite of the podman CLI.

Thanks, I tested podman-2.0.0-0.61.dev.git6aa802d.fc33 with
`podman ps --format '{{.Names}}'` and it is fine there.

Sorry I made a couple of mistakes earlier: firstly as you pointed out only `{{.Names}}` is accepted.
Additionally earlier I think I was testing with `podman images --format '{{.NAMES}}'`
and of course images do not have `name`: indeed `podman ps --format '{{.Names}}'` works fine
too on podman-1.9 too. Sorry for the noise and my confusion.

(It would be kind of nice if one could read off the placeholder strings from the output, but maybe uppercase stands out more.)
In a way making them case insensitive would not be so bad, though diverging from docker...
I think the original report still holds anyway (maybe `.Created` could be made an alias?).

Still failed:
```
$ podman --version
podman version 2.0.0
$ podman ps --format "table {{.Names}}\t{{.Status}}\t{{.RunningFor}}\t{{.Image}}\t{{.Ports}}"
Error: template: listContainers:1:39: executing "listContainers" at <.RunningFor>: can't evaluate field RunningFor in type containers.psReporter
$ podman ps --format "table {{.Names}}\t{{.Status}}\t{{.Image}}\t{{.Ports}}"
931f64f47dad-infra\tUp 19 minutes ago\tk8s.gcr.io/pause:3.2\t0.0.0.0:15432->5432/tcp, 0.0.0.0:9090->8080/tcp, 0.0.0.0:19200->9200/tcp, 0.0.0.0:5006->5006/tcp
unidata.docker-compose_unidata-elastic_1\tUp 19 minutes ago\tdocker.elastic.co/elasticsearch/elasticsearch:5.6.4\t
unidata.docker-compose_unidata-db_1\tUp 19 minutes ago\tdocker.io/library/postgres:11.8\t
unidata.docker-compose_unidata-app_1\tUp 18 minutes ago\tdocreg.taskdata.work/unidataapp:tag_r.5.2-ac-dev2\t
```
@jwhonce @mheon @baude Looks like RunningFor should be interpreted as StartedAt.  Is there a way in Go Format to translate these?
Given the way we do templates now, we can just add a function on the struct called RunningFor that returns StartedAt
We also need to translated he StartedAt to Human form.

@rhatdan @mheon So docker's documentation says it RunningFor returns 'Elapsed time since the container was started.' but its actual cli behavior returns time since created.. I'm leaning towards implementing what the docs say, but in that case, if the container has never been started, or has exited, is it okay for Runningfor to return "Not Running" ?
@ashley-cui When the Docker CLI and Documentation are different, we generally go with the thing the CLI actually does
